question: How does Amazon EC2 compare to running servers on premises?

choices.A: It is more expensive but offers more control.
choices.B: It is more flexible, cost-effective, and quicker to get started.
choices.C: It requires more time to set up and maintain.
choices.D: It is only useful for large businesses.

tip: Think speed, flexibility, and lower upfront effort.

explanations.A: Incorrect. Amazon EC2 is generally more cost-effective than running servers on premises.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon EC2 is more flexible, cost-effective, and faster to get started with than on-premises servers.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Amazon EC2 typically takes less time to provision and maintain.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Amazon EC2 is useful for businesses of all sizes.

question: What is multi-tenancy in Amazon EC2?

choices.A: Each virtual machine is isolated but shares resources from a host machine.
choices.B: Each customer has a dedicated server by default.
choices.C: Instances run in different Regions only.
choices.D: Only one virtual machine runs per host machine.

explanations.A: Correct. In Amazon EC2, virtual machines can share a host machine while remaining isolated from one another.
explanations.B: Incorrect. Dedicated hardware is not the default behavior in Amazon EC2.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Regions relate to geography, not multi-tenancy.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Multiple virtual machines can run on a single host.

question: What component enables resource sharing and isolation between Amazon EC2 instances on a host machine?

explanations.A: Incorrect. A firewall controls network traffic.
explanations.B: Correct. The hypervisor enables virtualization, resource sharing, and isolation between instances.
explanations.C: Incorrect. A load balancer distributes traffic, not host resources.
explanations.D: Incorrect. The operating system runs inside the instance.

question: Which Amazon EC2 instance family is BEST when workload performance is uncertain?

explanations.A: Correct. General purpose instances provide a balanced mix of compute, memory, and networking resources.
explanations.B: Incorrect. Compute optimized instances are best for CPU-intensive workloads.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Memory optimized instances are best for memory-intensive workloads.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Storage optimized instances are best for workloads requiring high local storage performance.

question: Which Amazon EC2 instance type is BEST for machine learning workloads that require hardware acceleration?

explanations.A: Incorrect. General purpose instances are balanced but not specialized for hardware acceleration.
explanations.B: Correct. Accelerated computing instances use hardware accelerators such as GPUs.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Storage optimized instances focus on local storage performance.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Reserved Instances are a pricing option, not an instance family.

question: When using Amazon EC2, what is the customer responsible for under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?
tip: You manage what is inside the instance.

explanations.A: Incorrect. AWS is responsible for physical hardware.
explanations.B: Incorrect. AWS manages the hypervisor.
explanations.C: Correct. Customers manage the guest operating system, applications, and many related configurations.
explanations.D: Incorrect. AWS handles physical data center security.

question: What is an Amazon Machine Image (AMI)?

choices.B: A template for operating system and software

explanations.A: Incorrect. An AMI is not a pricing model.
explanations.B: Correct. An AMI is a template that includes the operating system and optional preinstalled software.
explanations.C: Incorrect. An AMI is not a storage service.
explanations.D: Incorrect. An AMI is not a load balancer.

question: What is a primary advantage of using the AWS CLI over the AWS Management Console?

choices.D: It is intended only for one-time manual setup.

explanations.A: Incorrect. The Management Console is the visual interface.
explanations.B: Correct. The AWS CLI is especially useful for scripting and automation.
explanations.C: Incorrect. The AWS CLI works on multiple operating systems.
explanations.D: Incorrect. The AWS CLI is useful for both repeated and automated tasks.

question: What does horizontal scaling (scaling out) mean?

choices.A: Increase CPU or memory on a single instance

explanations.A: Incorrect. That is vertical scaling.
explanations.B: Correct. Horizontal scaling means adding more instances to handle more load.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Deleting instances reduces capacity.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Moving Regions is not the definition of horizontal scaling.

question: What is elasticity in AWS?

explanations.A: Incorrect. Elasticity is not manual by definition.
explanations.B: Correct. Elasticity is the ability to automatically adjust resources to match demand.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Fixed capacity is the opposite of elasticity.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Elasticity includes scaling down as well as scaling up.

question: What does Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling do?

choices.B: Adjusts the number of instances automatically

explanations.A: Incorrect. Storage changes are not the primary role of EC2 Auto Scaling.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically adjusts instance count based on demand or policies.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Traffic routing is handled by load balancers.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Log monitoring is typically handled by monitoring services such as CloudWatch.

question: What is the main role of Elastic Load Balancing (ELB)?

explanations.A: Incorrect. ELB does not create EC2 instances.
explanations.B: Correct. ELB distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets.
explanations.C: Incorrect. ELB does not store application data.
explanations.D: Incorrect. ELB is not a code monitoring service.

question: What is the main benefit of Amazon SQS?

choices.A: Immediate push notifications to many subscribers
choices.B: Stores messages until they are processed

explanations.A: Incorrect. That behavior is more aligned with Amazon SNS.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon SQS stores messages until they are retrieved and processed.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Amazon SQS introduces buffering rather than removing it.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Amazon SQS is a messaging service, not compute.

question: What is the key difference between Amazon SNS and Amazon SQS?

choices.A: Amazon SNS stores messages while Amazon SQS sends them instantly
choices.B: Amazon SNS pushes messages, while Amazon SQS queues them
choices.C: Amazon SNS is compute and Amazon SQS is storage

explanations.A: Incorrect. That reverses the roles of the two services.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon SNS is pub/sub push messaging, while Amazon SQS is message queuing.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Neither service is a compute service.
explanations.D: Incorrect. They serve different messaging patterns.

question: Which pricing option has no long-term commitment?

explanations.A: Incorrect. Reserved Instances require a commitment.
explanations.B: Incorrect. Savings Plans require a commitment.
explanations.C: Correct. On-Demand Instances require no long-term commitment.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Dedicated Hosts are not the standard no-commitment answer here.

question: Which pricing option is interruptible but often the cheapest?

explanations.A: Incorrect. On-Demand is flexible but not the cheapest.
explanations.B: Incorrect. Reserved pricing is discounted but not interruptible in the same way.
explanations.C: Correct. Spot Instances can provide major savings but can be interrupted by AWS.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Dedicated Hosts are not the cheapest option.

question: Which pricing option is generally best for predictable workloads?

explanations.A: Incorrect. Spot Instances are better for interruptible workloads.
explanations.B: Correct. Reserved Instances are commonly used for predictable workloads.
explanations.C: Incorrect. On-Demand is better for unpredictable or short-term usage.
explanations.D: Incorrect. ELB is not a pricing model.

question: Why deploy Amazon EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones?

explanations.A: Incorrect. Availability Zones do not increase CPU per instance.
explanations.B: Correct. Distributing instances across multiple Availability Zones improves resilience and high availability.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Multi-AZ design is for resilience, not guaranteed lower cost.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Internet speed is not the main reason.

question: What is a loosely coupled architecture?

choices.A: Components depend heavily on each other
choices.D: A design where all components share one database by default

explanations.A: Incorrect. That describes tightly coupled systems.
explanations.B: Correct. Loosely coupled architectures allow components to operate more independently.
explanations.C: Incorrect. That does not describe loose coupling.
explanations.D: Incorrect. A shared database does not define loosely coupled architecture.

question: A company needs to send one message to multiple subscribers, including email addresses, Lambda functions, and web servers. Which AWS service is the BEST choice?
tip: One message to many subscribers = pub/sub.

explanations.A: Incorrect. Amazon SQS is for queuing messages, not fan-out delivery to many subscribers.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon SNS is a publish-subscribe service that can deliver to multiple subscribers.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Amazon EC2 is a compute service.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Amazon EBS is block storage.

question: An application sends messages to a queue, and another application processes those messages later when it becomes available. Which AWS service is being used?

explanations.A: Incorrect. Amazon SNS is designed to push messages to subscribers.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon SQS stores messages in a queue until they are processed.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Elastic Load Balancing distributes traffic, not messages.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Amazon Route 53 is a DNS service.

question: Which statement BEST describes a loosely coupled architecture?

choices.A: Application components are directly dependent on each other, so if one fails, others are likely affected.
choices.B: Application components operate independently, so one component can fail without bringing down the whole system.
choices.C: All components must run on the same EC2 instance.
choices.D: Every application component must share the same database server.

explanations.A: Incorrect. That describes a tightly coupled architecture.
explanations.B: Correct. Loosely coupled components can continue working even if one component fails.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Running on the same instance is not required.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Sharing one database is not the defining trait.

question: Which AWS service is designed to route events from AWS services, custom applications, and third-party software to targets in an event-driven architecture?

explanations.A: Correct. Amazon EventBridge routes events from many sources to targets.
explanations.B: Incorrect. Amazon SQS is a queueing service.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Amazon SNS is a pub/sub service, but not a full event bus like EventBridge.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling manages instance scaling.

question: A development team wants to improve application resilience so that if one component fails, the rest of the application continues to run. Which architectural approach is BEST?
tip: Break the app into independent services.

explanations.A: Incorrect. Monolithic architectures often increase coupling between components.
explanations.B: Incorrect. Tightly coupled systems are more vulnerable to cascading failures.
explanations.C: Correct. Microservices architectures improve resilience by using more independent services.
explanations.D: Incorrect. A single-instance deployment creates more risk, not less.

question: Which TWO AWS services are designed for application messaging and communication between components? (Select TWO.)
tip: Think messaging services, not compute or traffic routing.

explanations.A: Correct. Amazon SQS is a message queuing service.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon SNS is a publish-subscribe messaging service.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Amazon EC2 is compute.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Elastic Load Balancing distributes traffic.

question: Which TWO characteristics describe a loosely coupled architecture? (Select TWO.)

choices.A: Components depend heavily on each other
choices.B: Failure in one component does not affect others
choices.D: All components must be deployed together

explanations.A: Incorrect. That describes tightly coupled systems.
explanations.B: Correct. Failures are less likely to spread to other components.
explanations.C: Correct. Components can operate more independently.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Deploying everything together is more aligned with tightly coupled designs.

question: Which TWO statements about Amazon SNS and Amazon SQS are correct? (Select TWO.)

choices.A: Amazon SNS is commonly used for fan-out messaging to multiple subscribers.
choices.B: Amazon SQS stores messages until they are processed.
choices.C: Amazon SNS is a block storage service.
choices.D: Amazon SQS is primarily used to host virtual machines.

explanations.A: Correct. Amazon SNS is commonly used to push messages to multiple subscribers.
explanations.B: Correct. Amazon SQS stores messages until a consumer processes them.
explanations.C: Incorrect. Amazon SNS is not a storage service.
explanations.D: Incorrect. Amazon SQS is not a compute or hosting service.
